desc:
The stream from the south meets the one flowing from the east. and merge into
one river heading north. A small trail fords the stream in that direction, or
you could step onto dry land to the west. A trail runs parallel to the water
there, from north to south.
name: Joining of Two Streams

desc:
Soft and quiet, the stream makes its way between a bluff that slopes away to the
east and a trail that runs through the plains to the west. The current is slow
and mild, coming from the south and joining another stream to the north.
name: Gentle Stream

desc:
The stream is rather narrow here, with its banks very high and its current
gentle and smooth. The water is very clear and cool to the touch and there is an
abundance of small fishes swimming in the water below. Plains stretch out to the
east and a trail runs to the west and south. The stream itself continues north.
name: Gentle Stream

desc:
Quite large and wide, the stream flows swiftly from the east to the west,
joining another stream in the western direction. Moss grows thick along the
bottom of the bluff that rises to the south, making it too slick to climb and
casting a greenish tint into the water when the sun shines brightly on it.
name: Stream close to a Joining

desc:
A small rivulet begins to expand here and turns into a full stream, flowing from
the east to the west. A bridle path follows the stream to the north and a rocky
bluff rises high in the air to the south, its sides too smooth and steep to
climb. Looking farther to the north, one can see the outline of jagged hills
rising above the horizon.
name: Expanding Rivulet

desc:
A tiny rivulet makes its way from the brook to the east and begins to widen to
the west. A bridle path is visible to the north and further on you can see
plains beginning in that direction. A large range of hills raises up to the
south.
name: Rivulet among the Hills

desc:
The quiet brook that flows from the south begins to expand in width and depth
here and actually becomes a small rivulet to the west. It cuts a path though the
plains with its strong current and is bordered by a path to the north. A sturdy,
wooden fence surrounding what looks to be grazing plains for the settlement
visible to the east prohibits travel in that direction.
name: Expanding Brook

desc:
Slow and gentle, the brook bends softly from the east to the north. Its current
seems to gradually be getting stronger, probably due to the excess run-off of
water from the surrounding hills, and the width of the brook definitly widens as
the brook continues on. The large hill range looms to both the east and south.
name: Curve in the Brook

desc:
The current of this brook is extremely slow and gentle and an abundance of fish
and other small water creatures take advantage of the barely flowing deep
waters, hiding in small crevices and under stones in the nearly still waters.
Lush plains lie to the north and the brook itself runs from the east to the
west.
name: Slowly Flowing Brook

desc:
Travelling through the plains, this trail is flat and wide, making travel upon
it very comfortable and allowing a traveller to survey the surrounding lands
without fear of stumbling. Looking to the west, an old campsite is visible and
two streams merge to form one to the east. The trail itself meets another trail
running crosswise to the north.
name: Trail along the Plains

desc:
This narrow trail makes its way between the smooth plains stretching away to the
west and a gentle stream that runs quietly to the east. The bank leading into
the stream is very slight and the water probably floods the path during the
rainier seasons of the year. The trail continues on straight north and south in
direction.
name: Trail along the Plains

desc:
Running parallel to the quiet stream which flows to the east, a small trail
begins here and travels on north through the fertile plains. A soft, grassy
field stretches out to the south and an area of smooth, unbroken plains begins
to the west.
name: Trail along the Plains

desc:
This trail runs through soft, bulging grass, which is slowly growing thinner and
sparser in all directions save east, where the trail continues. Like a great
brown shadow, a large patch of brushwood stains the fields to the south- west.
name: A Small Trail

desc:
Running alongside a small stream, the trail cuts a passage south across the
grasslands. As it runs north it becomes noticeably soggy and wet, but still
remains in good enough condition to support travel. Grasslands reach far to the
east, and desolate and barren fields lie to the west.
name: Trail along the Stream

desc:
Soft underfoot, this worn trail offers comfortable passage along the border of
the wilderness and the grasslands. Although primitive in its fashioning, it
seems to be well-used and might serve as a trading route between the people who
inhabit these lands. Its southern expanse grows ever wider, whereas to the north
the trail remains narrow and primitive. The soft trickling of the stream running
along the trail mixes with the sounds of wildlife, a serene oasis of nature
amongst the plains.
name: Well-trodden Trail across the Grasslands

desc:
The trail acts as a border between barren and brushy fields to the west and
soft, rolling grasslands to the east. The trail grows ever wider southwards as
it approaches a seemingly abandoned guardpost. A gentle stream runs by the
trail-side, accompanying it on its journey across this land.
name: Trail Approaching a Guardpost

desc:
The remnants of an old guardpost lie here in foreboding solitude. Debris of
collapsed barricades have been strewn across the trail, blocking it partially. A
broad, paved path leads east, past the greater of the barricades, into a
clearing among the mountain forest.
name: Forsaken Guardpost

desc:
This extremely level portion of the land is blanketed by a healthy mix of grass
and wildflowers. Clover in particular grows especially well, flourishing in the
rich, fertile soil and filling the air with its heavy scent. Smooth plains
stretch out to the south and grasslands continue to the north. It seems a
campsite has been constructed to the east but it is hard to tell from this
direction whether or not it is still in use.
name: Flat Grassland

desc:
Evidently once a favorite place for travellers to stop and rest on their
journeys, this old campsite appears to get little, if any, use now. Grass is
just beginning to grow again after being trampelled down by so many feet and
tents. A circle of stones surrounding the remains of a burnt-out fire
conveniently sits directly in the middle of the campsite. A trail is visible to
both the north and the east, flat grassland begins to the west, and smooth
plains expand out to the south.
name: Old Campsite

desc:
These smooth plains give a wide view of the surrounding lands, with little
vegetation except some short grass to get in the way. An old campsite lies
directly to the north and the plains themselves continue to the south. To the
east, a trail runs through the plains.
name: Smooth Plains

desc:
Growing thick and plentiful, short plains grass covers every inch of ground,
creating a vast sea of green vegetation that flows to the north for quite some
ways. Brush-filled plains begin to the south and a trail winds through the
plains to the east.
name: Smooth Plains

desc:
Long patches of brushwood stretch out from the dense undergrowth south, but they
quickly thin out and fade into the bulging grass of the fields. Travelling south
seems not only uncomfortable but also unwise since the fields to the east offer
a far better route of passage.
name: Brush-spotted Plains

desc:
Rough brushwood surrounds you, blotting out the ground with its thick branches.
Rattling noises, the crunching of twigs underfoot and the rustling of leaves can
be heard from all around. Many small creatures must be scuttling about nearby,
or maybe the brush is just shifting around in the wind. It stretches even
further west and north, while to the south and east lie barren and wild lands.
name: Thick, Unrelenting Brushwood

desc:
Brown-stained grass and twisted shrubs blend together to form a truly hostile
environment. The northern horizon is stained by large patches of brushwood
amidst the plains. The southern fields seem even more desolate, but the
vegetation that does exist there looks greener, and healthier in a way.
name: Inhospitable Wilderness

desc:
The harsh and barren wilderness stretches north into rough brushwood. Desolate
fields lie all around you, but far to the south, past a bleak trail, the grass
grows thick and full. Faint slithering impressions lead in all directions,
bearing evidence of existing wildlife in this infertile surrounding.
name: Barren Wilderness

desc:
The trail's condition worsens as it strays into the brushwood westward - a real
forsaken wilderness. This is clearly not a path often taken by the people of
Dunland; seeing the hostile brushwood sway and rattle in the wind shows why. A
more pleasant path can be seen to the east, at a crossing of trails.
name: Bleak Trail

desc:
Three trails intertwine to form a crossing outside an old guardpost. A bleak
trail leads west into the wilderness and shows little promise of comfortable
travelling. The southern trail seems to widen as it cuts through the plains,
perhaps a sign of more frequent use.
name: Crossing West of a Guardpost

desc:
Sparsely vegetated fields turn even more barren as they stretch westwards. A
trail running along the south and east borders of the fields seems to lead to
lands less wasted and lifeless, sheltered somewhat by the Misty Mountains. Far
to the east you see that great mountain range tower into the skies.
name: Barren Fields

desc:
Dusty fields lie at the eastern border of widespread wildlands. Just east of
here a trail takes a north-south route across the grasslands, running alongside
a small stream. The fields, growing richer as they draw north and more desolate
in the south, continue along the outskirts of the vast windswept plains to the
west.
name: Fields Bordering the Wilderness

desc:
Small patches of brushwood stretch out from the west, where the ground is
completely covered by the thick undergrowth. Gazing across the western horizon
you see naught which would justify passage through the dense vegetation. A far
easier and more comfortable route of travel lies east, in the form of a trail
running north-south.
name: Wide Plains Bordering Thick Brushwood

desc:
The trail struggles to make its way through the surrounding wilderness, here
faltering and making an abrupt turn to the north. Southwards lie the wide open
grasslands of Enedwaith, with sporadic patches of brushwood and shrubbery. To
the east the trail grows slightly stronger, with less vegetation to overwhelm
it.
name: Faint Trail through the Wilderness

desc:
The trail finally succumbs to the wilderness and decay which surrounds it and
becomes nothing but a faint outline and vanishes. The wilderness spreads out in
every direction, save the trail leading south. East is a spur from the main
mountain range, vast in its own right.
name: Overgrown Trail

desc:
Little hope resides here. The wind plagues the open wildlands, and nowhere is
shelter to be found. Large patches of thick brushwood lie to the north, like
great brown stains on the barren fields. Faint, but still discernible, a trail
runs to the south.
name: Sombre Wilderness

desc:
The ground itself becomes impossible to see as the thick brushwood rolls out
across the plains. The brush rattles and sways in the wind, their naked twiggy
fingers stretching out towards you. Northwards part of the brush yields and
opens up, letting the ground show once again. To the south it borders the wild
plains of Enedwaith, stretching endlessly.
name: Unyielding Brushwood

desc:
Spots of brushwood cover the plains, but as they draw north the spots grow fewer
and further apart. In the south the brush grows very dense and completely
smoulders the ground it has sprung out of. Eastward lie rolling grasslands, and
beyond them a huge mountain range on the horizon.
name: Brushy Plains

desc:
A short, tough grass flourishes in the fertile soil of these plains, clinging
low to the ground and creating the appearance of an unbroken sea of green grass
that continues both north and east. The plains to the south seem to break up and
become bushier.
name: Smooth Plains

desc:
Smooth, unbroken plains extend to both the south and east, covered with a
mixture of dense, tough grass and short, wild grains. Flat grasslands open up to
the north as far as the eye can see.
name: Smooth Plains

desc:
The spur of hills protrudes from the grasslands, in contrast to the flat plains
which surround them. As they draw closer to their parent mountain range, their
height grows and the land become barren and cold. Elevated above the great plain
of Enedwaith, the hills give a good view over the grassy lands. However that
wide and somewhat monotonous landscape makes it hard to focus attention on
anything in particular, as the swathe of green merges into a whole.
name: Protruding Hills

desc:
Rough, uneven plains stretch out here, covered by a short, tough grass which
seems to grow lusher and more fertile to the east. A large hill range extends to
both the south and west and a brook is flowing slowly through the plains and
hills to the north.
name: Uneven Plains

desc:
Extremely rough hills make travel arduous with loose stone and sharp inclines
taking all of one's time and energy. The ground is practically bare of all
vegetation except a few clumps of short grass here and there along the
hillsides. The hills continue on to both the south and west and plains begin to
the east.
name: Rough Hills

desc:
As the hills stretch further north, the western spur-ends of the range slump
swiftly. They come to an abrupt ending west of here, the grasslands taking over
dominance of the landscape. East and south the foothills grow, as they run
nearer to the vast mountains. Still, they remain accessible, mere lumps in the
ground compared to those snow-tipped behemoths.
name: Protruding Hills

desc:
A range of hills resides east of here. The grasslands themselves are riddled
with sharp, pointy rocks and bleak slabs of stone. Further to the south the
grasslands are unscathed by the cold, hard rocks, becoming lusher in stark
contrast to the mountains.
name: Rocky Grasslands

desc:
The steep incline and uneven ground of these hills conspire to give a traveller
difficulty passing through them. The range continues heading on to the north or
you could make your way through the plains to the west or the grasslands to the
south.
name: Rough Hills

desc:
This hill range is wedged between the bluff to the west, a small rivulet to the
north, and the brook that flows to the east. The hills seem to extend to the
south for miles and are covered with a short, tough grass. Many loose rocks make
travel in this area somewhat treacherous.
name: Cornering Hills

desc:
From the top of this bluff you can see for miles in every direction, the land
stretching out before you in a sea of colour and variety. Looking north, one can
see a small rivulet below heading east, the sound of its water echoing off the
side of the bluff. Directly to the east, a series of hills rises up but you
would have to climb back down the bluff and enter them from the south-west. Far
to the north, a large forest is visible and a watch tower can be seen quite
clearly in the north-west from this height. A large nest has been built into a
crevice in the rocks of this bluff.
name: Bluff in Front of the Rivulet

desc:
Sloping from a southern angle, this rocky bluff overlooks a stream running at
its base to the north and is bordered to the south by a series of rugged plains.
One could climb higher onto the bluff to the east and get a better view of the
land from there. It looks too difficult to scramble down the side of the bluff
to either the west or north.
name: Sloping Bluff

desc:
Clods of earth and short tufts of grass break up the land in these plains. It
looks as though a herd of animals find this a popular grazing spot and the
ground certainly shows signs of being torn up by their hooves as they make their
way across the plains. A bluff slopes upwards to the north and more plains
continue to the south.
name: Rugged Plains

desc:
Broken plains covered with a thick blanket of short grass and tall grains
stretch from here on to the west. Loose stone and clods of dirt make passage
across these plains difficult and time consuming. You can climb up to the top of
the bluff to the north or enter the rocky grasslands that stretch out to the
south.
name: Rugged Plains

desc:
Tufts of soft, lush grass blend with sharp-edged rocks and stones to form a
craggy and harsh landscape. As the lands stretch south, the rocks become sparse
until finally all that remains is the soft and gentle grass. The western grounds
are partially drenched in water, making the soil soft and slippery.
name: Rocky Grasslands

desc:
A nearby stream fills the air with its soft trickling sounds. To the west you
can espy a trail travelling in a north-south direction across the plains. Vast,
unkempt grasslands stretch far southeast, running up to the very foothills of a
grand range of mountain.
name: Soggy Plains

desc:
The grass grows thick and rich here, nestled at the base of the mountains and
flanked in the north by an expanse of hills. The mountainside makes a steep
climb and quickly rises into the sky. Vast grasslands stretch onwards to the
north and west.
name: Grasslands below the Towering Mountains

desc:
As the towering mountains become more of a noticeable presence in the south, the
grasslands finally yield their dominance of the landscape and give way to a
forest running along the base of the mountain range. Far to the north and west
the rich, green grass still remains the predominant feature of the landscape, to
the edge of your vision.
name: Wide Grasslands

desc:
A light forest runs along the southern border of the grasslands, out of place
with the monotony of the surrounding plains. The snow-capped heights of the
mountains to the south thrust high into the sky, their presence overwhelming the
surrounding landscape. Somehow, when this close everything else appears quite
insignificant when faced with these grand giants of nature.
name: Wide Grasslands

desc:
As the grasslands draw nearer to the southern forest, the grass grows thicker
and darker, and sporadic shrubbery helps the transition from fields into
woodland. Past the hilly forest lie the rising mountains, their broken angles
and sharp edges making them appear impossible to scale.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Like a monotonous green blanket, smothering all shape and variation, the vast
grasslands roll out across the eastern landscape. Here and there the grass forms
low banks, which look like small ripples on this vast and serene ocean of
greenery.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Just west of here a trail barrels its way south across the plains, closely
accompanied by the gentle trickle of a small stream. The entire landscape to the
east blends into a monotonous green carpet of grass, occasionally varied by the
odd bank or slope. The lowlands take an abrupt end in the southeast, where a
small forest guards the base of the vast mountain range.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass rolls out all around you, endlessly. The monotony of the featureless
green landscape would make it hard not to lose your bearings, if not for the
massive hulking mountain range east of here. A landmark of that size, so close,
is impossible to overlook.
name: Sweeping Grasslands

desc:
The thick blanket of soft grass stretches across the landscape, bathing the
surroundings in a lush green hue. Far across the southern expanse of the
grasslands great mountains stand tall and proud, breaking the monotony of the
grassy lowlands.
name: Sweeping Grasslands

desc:
The lush green grasslands roll out towards the western horizon, flanked by a
wide range of hills to the east. The sharp and slumped hillside looks fairly
impossible to scale, the side of the bluff being sheer if not particularly high.
name: Sweeping Grasslands

desc:
An expanse of hills stretch out from the mountains across the grasslands. The
steep inclines and rocky hillsides make this terrain cumbersome to travel in,
and not at all appeasing to the eye with its cold and craggy landscape. West the
cliff is too steep to descend.
name: Expanse of the Hills

desc:
Green fields cower at the base of the overwhelming mountains, basked in a faint
grey shadow. Further south, moving away from the mountains, the fields grow to
be exceptionally lush, the wildlife flourishing to a point where the landscape
seems absolutely riddled with small animals.
name: Grassy Fields below the Vast Mountain Range

desc:
A bulky overhang blots out the sky above, casting a thick, grey shadow onto the
ground below. Every now and then small pebbles tumble down from above, as if the
mountainside above was being stirred by something. The protrusion of the
overhang is so great that one would question the stability of the rock, and
indeed one could speculate about one's personal safety standing under it.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
A vast array of rocks of all different sizes and shapes impale the ground,
making it craggy and cumbersome to travel upon. Rich grasslands riddled with
waist-high grass roll out across the southern plateau, as far as the eye can
tell. Northwards the gloomy mountains reach high into the sky above your head,
impossible to climb and vast to behold.
name: Boulder-strewn Plains

desc:
Large chunks of rock impale the ground, as if they had been thrust down by a
great force. The sharp boulders prove quite a hindrance for travellers trying to
cross this field, slowing the pace of safe travel considerably. To the north the
cliff side is impassable, towering immeasurably above you. West the ground
breaks off sharply, the drop too steep to descend.
name: Boulder-strewn Plains

desc:
The face of the mountain is almost unnaturally smooth as if it indeed had been
wrought into such a shape, but who is to say what shapes nature decides to take?
Thrust high into the sky a lone ledge protrudes slightly from the mountainside,
making it a long drop for anyone unfortunate enough to stray over its edge. A
plateau rising to the east is more rugged and uneven-looking than the mountain
to the north; perhaps this land settled downwards at some time in the past.
name: Below an Overwhelming Precipice

desc:
A huge boulder cleaves the ground here, lying almost lopsidedly as it balances
on its edge. Nevertheless it seems firmly implanted into the rocky soil, as if
fallen from a great height. A steep hillside rises north and west of you, while
east all is passage is blocked by a small spur of solid rock. Southwards the
landscape changes shape into a rocky heath with a sharp and harsh texture.
name: Rift Valley

desc:
The ground is serrated by sharp-edged rocks and large, bulky boulders. The
mountainside slowly makes its way up into the sky to the east, but although not
that steep, its harsh and broken surface makes it virtually impossible to scale.
The ground falls off sharply to the south creating a valley, near hidden within
the undulating and rocky expanse of plains. To your north there lies an old
guard-post, its barricades broken and seemingly abandoned.
name: Rocky Plains at the Base of the Mountain

desc:
The trail traverses across the wide plains, a small hunting trail that is not
much used. The ground falls away sharply to the east, creating a sheltered
valley in the otherwise open plains, and south into a narrow crevice. As the
trail continues south-west it widens continuously, and almost seems to gain pace
as it swiftly cuts a passage through the lands.
name: Traversing Trail across the Plains

desc:
Flanked by brushy grasslands to the west and rocky, undulating plains in the
east, the trail accesses a crossing of trails which lies north, but also cuts a
passage south across the wide lands. East of here lies a towering spur of the
Misties, a solemn and massive impression on the eastern skyline.
name: Trail South of a Crossing

desc:
The thick grass struggles to ward off the growth of brushwood, which seems to
originate from the south. A bleak trail makes its presence duly noticed in the
north, but it doesn't seem quite as comfortable as the much wider trail running
along the eastern border of the grassland.
name: Brushy Grasslands

desc:
Long patches of brush reach northwards towards a faint trail, but falter and
fade away into the soft grass. Further to the east the brush remains sparse,
succumbing there to the denser waving undergrowth. Southwards it gains strength
and grows ominously dense.
name: Brushy Grasslands

desc:
Thick brushwood blots out the ground entirely, save a few bald spots where the
brown-stained grass manages to break through. In the south a dense thicket
stands tall, a rare sight in these lowlands, but it does not look as healthy as
the surrounding landscape. The grim bulk of the tall trees quench all light,
making it a dark and dreary place. A trail runs swiftly across the plains in the
east, a welcome sight amidst the harsh brushwood.
name: Widespread Brushwood

desc:
Patches of brush stretch out towards the western border of the trail, like long
arms trying to snatch unwary travellers and drag them into the thick brushwood
in the west. The southern route of the trail runs unhindered across the smooth
plains, making very few turns. Northwards the trail bends slightly as it
struggles to avoid the outstretched patches of brush.
name: Traversing Trail across the Plains

desc:
Large, bulky trees cast their shadows upon you, surrounding you with darkness.
The air in this murky thicket grows suffocatingly thick, weighing upon you like
a heavy burden on your travels. Sudden gusts of fresh air send mournful shivers
through the dark tree-tops, filling the thicket with a welcome breeze.
name: In the Shadow of Some Trees

desc:
A dense thicket of trees lies nestled on the western border of the trail, a
rarity indeed in these almost shapeless grasslands. The trail swiftly travels
south, bending slightly as it passes some undulations in the grasslands, barely
missing a deep crevice in ground just east of here. The northern route from here
slowly but steadily runs across the lowlands, heading towards the end of a vast
mountain spur.
name: Trail Stretching across the Wide Plains

desc:
A narrow crevice runs for some way into the valley's side, only to narrow and
end abruptly. The steep sides of the crevice rise up above your head, almost
touching in parts and ensuring no sunlight can reach to the base. Echoes of
subsoil water dripping down from the broken angles of the crevice walls break
the ominous silence.
name: Dark Crevice

desc:
The wide stretch of grassy land in the valley gives way here, succumbing to its
craggy surroundings. A low range of hills lies south, past a small cluster of
rocky fields, creating a natural rift valley reaching out further north and
south. To the west a dark crevice lies below the rising plains and outstretched
fields.
name: Rift Valley

desc:
The soft peat here draws to an end to the west, opening into a rocky valley
floor. East of here, atop the higher plains, the grasslands have grown wild and
unkempt, with thick waist-high grass and flourishing wildlife. The northern
mountain range rests majestically at the end of the grasslands, surveying the
lands solemnly.
name: Valley Floor

desc:
A stretch of waist-high grass runs like a border between the rocky plains to the
north and the grasslands. Beyond the grass, at the edge of your vision, a gentle
heath traverses across the landscape, edging along below a low range of hills.
To your west the ground falls off rapidly, forming a natural valley, and to the
north, silent and ominous mountains break the skyline with their high, snow-clad
peaks.
name: Grassy Plateau

desc:
Waist-high, rich grass softly sways in gentle gusts of wind here. The serene
setting is occasionally disturbed as animals scurry about and stir up rattling
noises within the thick grass beds. A vast mountain range lies not far to the
north, its gargantuan presence dominating the landscape.
name: High Grass

desc:
Small well-trodden trails, barely noticeable in the rich grass, run south over
the tiny rocky spur there, and east through some meadows. The richness of the
wildlife and the many trails lead to the conclusion that these fields serve as
hunting grounds. The northern horizon is completely dominated by great peaks of
the mountain-range, itself only a spur from the main body of the vastness that
is the Misty Mountains.
name: Hunting Fields

desc:
The wildlife thrives and flourishes amidst the rich fields, sheltered slightly
from the great plateau winds by the Misty Mountains to the east. North of here
the ground is less fertile, becoming increasingly rocky as it draws closer to a
spur of those mountains. Gentle meadows run playfully across the southern
landscape, filling the air with a faint heathery scent.
name: Hunting Fields

desc:
The heathery meadows draw further east, flanked by fields on their northern
side. The peat continues to the west, like a vast belt across the landscape. A
grand spur of mountains to the north, thrusting out from the main range, ends
the expanse of fields in that direction, although doubtless they continue on the
other side.
name: Heathery Meadow

desc:
An ingenuously camouflaged windbreak, covered with turf and grass, offers
shelter and comfort for the weary. A peculiar fireplace wrought in front of the
windbreak draws attention. Stones have been stacked in intricate ways to channel
its smoke, thus avoiding a large column rising up and attracting unwanted
attention.
name: Secluded Campsite

desc:
A long stretch of fields blaze a passage through the surrounding high grass and
peat. As it draws further east the terrain turns into a blend of heathery
meadows and grassy fields, which runs all the way to the base of the towering
mountain range in the east.
name: Blazing Fields

desc:
Banks of grass gently swoop down from the hills to the south, like rolling
waves. Further to the north the grass grows thick and high, perfect for the
hunting of prey. A long belt of heath rolls in from the north and trails away to
the south-east, only ended by the cliff-side of a hilly spur.
name: Sloping Grasslands

desc:
A low range of hills bulges out from the grasslands in the south, where the
stretching peat turns slightly northwards into. West and north the land falls
down sharply into a valley, while east it rises further. The route up there is
slightly slippery, with many loose stones ready to trip the climber.
name: Isolated Heathland

desc:
Rocky plains stretch out towards the west, where the craggy soil becomes soft
and wholesome. A low range of hills rise and fall to create a small valley to
the north, offering seclusion from the open lowlands. Soft peat stretches like a
long tail across the valley mouth here, a strange and extremely noticeable
exception in the landscape.
name: Mouth of the Valley

desc:
The plains incline slightly as they rise to meet a low range of hills in the
south. The ground becomes craggy and barren in the east as it falls into a
valley, while further east the terrain becomes boggy and sloped. Far beyond all
that is a vast mountain range, dominating the horizon. A swift trail gently runs
over slopes and banks west of here, while north the land falls into a sudden
crevice.
name: Wide, Tranquil Plains

desc:
The trail disappears around the corner of a murky forest southwest of here, but
runs steadily onwards north without any hindrance, towards the end of a grand
range of mountains. Those are but a spur of the Misty Mountains, but still they
tower into the skyline, a stark contrast to the surrounding lowlands and grassy
fields.
name: Swiftly Bending Trail

desc:
A murky thicket, drenched in darkness, stands like a persistent obstacle in the
north. The trail is forced to bend eastwards to avoid the bulk of the trees as
it travels north. Southwards the trail runs freely across the plains, offering
safe passage through the lands.
name: Swift Trail

desc:
Climbing further into the mountains seems undoable as they rise up into the sky.
The ledge you have reached offers little protection from the tormenting winds
that lash out at you as you cling to the rock, and as you grope around for a
better grip you notice a wide shaft in the face of the mountain.
name: Climbing the Steep Mountainside

desc:
The trees nestle among rocks and crevices at the base of the mountains. Swiftly
the Misties rise into the sky, making it a seemingly impossible climb, although
a relatively smooth part of rock lies just to the south. An experienced climber
might be able to gain some footing there, but the chances of reaching the peaks
look meagre.
name: Mountainous Forest

desc:
Wide and unkempt grasslands roll out before you, reaching far to the north and
east. Great mountains rise into the southern skyline, and a thick forest runs
along their base. To the west a trail cuts across the plains, like a borderline
between the rich grasslands and wide-stretched wilderness.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Clear water springs out from the mountainside and softly trickles down into a
clearing in the forest. The steep rise of the mountainside makes travel south or
east impossible. The forestline continues west but halts at the border of the
wide grasslands.
name: Clearing at a Mountain Spring

desc:
The treeline grows thin as it travels north to meet the grasslands but thickens
in the opposite direction. The rocky forest floor is slightly inclined, and
doesn't provide easy footing for travellers taking a southbound turn. A small
clearing lies east, and a soft, trickling sound beckons you thither.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Grasslands

desc:
A light forest borders the rolling grasslands but as it draws closer to the
mountains in the south it thickens, outlining the base of the thrusting spur.
The range of peaks mountains rise high into the skyline, creating an impassable
hindrance to travel in that direction.
name: Light Forest Bordering the Grasslands

desc:
The forestline runs up to the very base of the mountains, and some trees even
sprout out in odd angles from the rockface itself. Far north, through the bulk
of the trees, vast grasslands roll out across the horizon. The forestline
continues both east and west, grabbing all available land between the rocks.
name: Forest at the Base of the Mountains

desc:
The forestline runs up to the very base of the mountains, and some trees even
sprout out in odd angles from the rockface itself. Far north, through the bulk
of the trees, vast grasslands roll out across the horizon. The forestline
continues both east and west.
name: Mountainous Forest

desc:
As the trail travels south it seems to grow ever wider, winding its way south to
approach the remnants of a long since forgotten road. Here and there gravel has
been strewn across the trail, but it does not in any way hinder travel, nor does
it affect the difficulty of movement.
name: Widening Trail

desc:
The field is riddled with loose gravel, which shifts as it is trodden upon,
making the footing unsteady and tiresome. A widening trail lies nearby to the
west, where it offers comfortable passage north or south across the lands. A low
range of hills rises at the eastern border of the field and stretches further
east across the heath.
name: Gravelly Field

desc:
A low range of slumped hills bulge out from the soft plains. To the northeast
the hills drop and form a small valley which lies sheltered by its flanking
hillsides. Gazing out from the raised hills you see a swift trail traversing
across the north-western plains.
name: Slumped Hills

desc:
The surrounding hills make this campsite ideal, sheltering travellers not only
from harsh winds but also providing cover from onlooking eyes, properties which
are hard to find amidst the wide open plains. The resources of the campsite
itself are meagre - all that remain are a burnt out fire and a few aged logs
suitable for resting on.
name: Small Camp Sheltered by the Hills

desc:
A low range of hills rise from the surrounding heath and boggy grounds, but
swiftly fall into a small depression to the west. On the far side the hills
continue. Far away to the east a vast mountain range makes its presence well
noted, a giant presence on the horizon. To the north runs a stretch of flat
plains, surrounded to the west and north by steep drops, and on the east by a
high slope.
name: Low Range of Hills

desc:
The hillside, with its moss-covered protrusions and soft coating of grass and
bog, becomes increasingly steep to the west. To the north it slants downwards
along a grassy plateau before the Misty Mountains. The bog diminishes along the
sides of the hill, until it disappears in the grasslands below.
name: Boggy Hillside

desc:
Soft peat rolls out like a warm blanket, or a coating, over the eastern lands.
The western ground swiftly rises into a boggy hillside, whereas the northern
lands are flat as can be, with wholesome, green grass. East and north is a rich
plateau in the arms of two gigantic spurs from the Misty Mountains.
name: Soft Peat

desc:
Like a belt, the peat stretches east and west but in the north it gives way to
grassy fields full of wildlife. A tower stands like a lone sentry to the
south-east, on the end of a hilly spur thrust from the main range, while just
south of here a valley falls away. Along the horizon the vast mountains curve
around, dominating all the landscape to the north and east.
name: Soft Peat

desc:
The constant scent of heather is here, across the soft peat. Alluring meadows
lie to the north-east, and when the wind comes from that direction the scent of
heather becomes even more noticeable. A grim tower high above you breaks the
southern skyline, silently surveying the lands from its hilly outcrop.
name: Heather-scented Peat

desc:
The terrain continues to slowly rise here toward the Misty Mountains in the
northeast. There is a good view over the fields and plains-land bordering the
mountains, thus the tower to the north. It is now partially ruined and covered
in moss, but would probably offer some protection to those defending it.
name: Before a Crumbled Tower

desc:
This lush valley is as isolated as they come, with the only entrance being
through a narrow ravine to the south. A sheer crescent-shaped cliff bars all
other directions. The valley is teeming with wildlife, the undergrowth about
waist-height. In a few places there is soft, short grass with patches of wild
flowers, gentle pastels with bright purple and blues thrown in for a colourful
combination. Scattered about the valley a few trees have taken root, mostly oak
but with a few maples and some apple trees.
name: A Secluded Deep Valley

desc:
This is a vast plain of waist high wild wheat, tips swaying in unison with the
gentle breeze. In the near distance the wheat give off a heather-grey hue, while
further away the fields appear almost black. On the eastern edge of this field
there are some trees bordering a crescent-shaped cliff, below which is a
secluded valley. On the far side of this gap, a ridge of hills rise, with an old
tower visible atop their edge.
name: A High Plain Bordering a Cliff

desc:
The hills have smoothed out to this level expanse of land, covered in a light
waist-high field of wild wheat mixed with some tall grass. Besides the grass the
land is mostly without growth, except for a few oak trees. Looking about from
the edges of this plain, the grasslands continue on to the south, but north give
away to broken rocky lands.
name: High Plain

desc:
Clustered together like a bunch of grapes, these small foothills rise higher
above the plains than the rolling plains you can discern to the west. Upon the
slopes the grass becomes short and sparse, as the roots have a difficult time
taking hold in the soft earth. On the steepest parts there is no vegetation,
just dry brown earth. North the hills grow above the plains, like an arm from
the Misty Mountains.
name: Cascading Downs

desc:
The earth flows over some mild downs. These gentle mounds, tiny foothills to the
rougher land to the north, are teeming with wild grass about the height of a
man's knee. To the east there is a faint outline of more hills leading into the
main range of mountains. South, the hills mellow out into a vast prairie, a
windswept and open land.
name: Gentle Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The land, which is virtually flat here, rises upwards to the north into a range
of exposed hills. Almost like a line extending from the Misty Mountains, the
land is split between the ragged and broken plateau to the north, and the flat
lands stretching down to the Gap of Rohan.
name: Before Some Small Hills

desc:
Here there are small clumps of grass, interspersed with beds of gravel to the
south and west. The gravel comes from years of erosion on the unmaintained road
there. To the east the grass becomes thick underfoot, with a few solitary trees
breaking up the monotonous skyline. In the distance rise the ends of two great
mountain ranges: to the northeast the Misty Mountains, and to the southeast the
White Mountains.
name: Short Sparse Grasslands

desc:
The cracked paving stones are few and far between here, barely recognisable as
the remains of a once great road. The stones, which it seems were once smoothly
cut and joined together, are now uneven and broken into bits of gravel. There
are some jagged little edges sticking up from the dirt of the remaining larger
stones, making the route a slow one. East the end of the Misty Mountains can be
seen rising on the horizon, their snowy peaks overlooking the Gap of Rohan.
name: A Decayed Road

desc:
The large meadows come to an abrupt halt here as a barely noticeable trail of
stone debris makes its way through them. Though in very poor shape, there are
still hints that a larger road formerly passed through here. What remains now is
almost completely overgrown by grass and vegetation.
name: A Few Stones Resembling a Road

desc:
Almost completely hidden by the grass is an ancient road. It is being taken back
by nature gradually, although a few large paving stones remain. They are covered
in a light moss, due to the non-use of this route. Between some of them coarse
grass and unruly weeds have grown, breaking the slabs into loose pieces of
gravel.
name: Fading Remains of a Road

desc:
The ground here is covered in short green grass, interspersed with patches of
white daisies. There are a few trees here and there - mostly oak, with a few
birch. These grasslands continue all around, interrupted to the south and west
by the remnants of an ancient road.
name: Short Grasslands along the Road

desc:
Here the land is mostly flat, and is covered in soft, bright green grass. There
are patches of colourful wildflowers, breaking up the monotony of the land for a
short while. Around these flowers are fresh mounds of dirt, pushed up by some
busy animal digging. North the flat plains flow into some rolling grasslands,
while elsewhere the flat plains continue.
name: Teeming Grassland

desc:
The waist-high grass is beginning to merge into fields of wild wheat here, the
natural crops becoming more prevalent the further east you look. There are some
periodic piles of rubble, the remains of abandoned Dunlending homesteads. They
built their farms far from the great Numenorean road, avoiding the travellers
that used that route.
name: Gentle Rolling Plains

desc:
The lush grass merges into fields of wild wheat on this long, gradual, slope,
some of it dry and brittle from the poor soil. It seems the cutting winds have
stripped the earth of its topsoil, leaving bare a mostly rocky ground. East the
grass grows strongly again, before falling away into a valley.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
This faint slope is covered with wild wheat and patches of wild flowers. In some
spots the slope is barren, and the earth is visible. There are several mounds of
fresh dirt and some other holes dug into the hillside by the animal life that
inhabits this region. Natural rock formations block the way not far east of
here, rising above the surrounding plains. South, the ground levels out into a
vast prairie of wild wheat.
name: A Sloping Hillside

desc:
This rocky valley floor twists and turns in various different directions and is
extremely narrow, which makes travel through here tedious. Green moss grows on
the cliff walls, which rear up to the east. The southern side of the ravine is
not steep, eventually disappearing into the grassy fields. The ground is mostly
bare rock, with short grass in patches. The ravine continues on to the north,
with the walls increasing into sheer unclimbable cliffs.
name: A Narrow Valley

desc:
The trail starts to slowly zigzag its way up the ridge to the decayed watch
tower visible above. The track sometimes wanders around gigantic rock outcrops,
sticking out from the hillside like knife edges. The ledge here is at times so
narrow that it would be almost impossible for a wagon to make its way up. Yet it
has been done at some time, since wagon ruts are still evident in the soil.
name: A Narrow Trail up a Ridge

desc:
The ground is littered with rocks here. A track from the west meanders around
various detours, making its way onto a trail that goes up the steep ridge to the
north. There are places where the track used to be, but large boulders now
cover. Where the ground is not covered with rocks there is short, sparse grass
and in places craters of varying sizes. Above, a long distance to the north
there is a tower of some sort visible on the end of the spur of hills. South of
here the ground slopes downward to the outskirts of some farm.
name: Rocky Flats

desc:
The prairies to the west give way to gravel and large rocks here, with only
tufts of greenery here and there. To the north the land drops away slightly,
forming a valley and then a ravine. South a line in the ground continues on, the
remains of a dried-up riverbed. Also meandering through here is an old forgotten
track that seems to be headed to the tower visible on a hilltop to the
northeast.
name: End of the Prairie

desc:
An old trail is faintly visible here, with wagon ruts in parts. Near this track
is huge swathes of wild wheat, dry and brittle. South of here there are many
stray bushes taking root in amongst the brambles and vines, making it a near
impenetrable tangle. There are several small tunnels into the thorny mess, but
only for small creatures. North, the terrain breaks into a gentle slope leading
up to a high plain. East and west the prairieland continues.
name: Everlasting Prairie

desc:
This prairie continues in every direction, except to the north where it rises on
a gentle slope. The area is abundant with wildlife, with patches of bright wild
flowers and a few trees scattered about. To the south-west a road is just
visible cutting through wetlands on a raised causeway.
name: Endless Prairie

desc:
This continuos flat land is teeming with grass and small wildlife. Most of the
animals can be seen and not heard, rustling about the undergrowth. The grass
grows abundantly, about waist high in most places, hiding the creatures from
view. This vast expanse of land continues as far as the eye can see, the huge
expanse of the Misty Mountains in full view on the horizon.
name: Vast Prairie

desc:
The lush, abundant plains are constant all around here, varying from bushes and
undergrowth nearer the road to open grasslands further away. Just before the old
road to the south, the land falls into a natural depression, filled with thick
brush. Beyond it is a raised causeway, the road originally built above the lands
to protect it from flooding.
name: Verdant Flatlands

desc:
The land here is bordered by an old road, which cuts across the grasslands. The
ground is covered with lush but short grass, beaten down by the powerful winds
that can sweep across the land. To the north, the ground steadily rises towards
a series of small rolling hills. The flat plains continue all around from here,
becoming more vibrant to the east.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Crumbled away over the ages since the route was last maintained, the road is now
nothing more than loose gravel and countless cracked paving stones. The line it
follows is unmistakable though: from south-east to north-west it goes, across
the plains of Enedwaith.
name: Fading Remains of a Road

desc:
The gentle, rolling plains have levelled out into this meadow of very thick,
soft grass. Herds of heavy beasts must graze here at some time in the year,
judging by the swathes of trampled grass. To the east are the last remnants of a
road, which originally travelled north-west to south-east. Further still is the
outline of a mountain range, dominating the horizon.
name: A Lush Green Meadow

desc:
The land steadily rises here to the south. The grass is short, soft and lush, a
natural place for the animal herds that have trampled it. To the east are the
remains of a road, originally wide and proud but now reduced to rubble. It runs
north to south there, built slightly above the surrounding fields.
name: A Gentle Grassy Slope

desc:
The road here has been almost completely overgrown with grass, soon to be
forgotten and lost in history. It was once maintained by the armies of Gondor
and Arnor, but no mason has mended it for many centuries now. Southeast it rises
up onto an earthen causeway, carrying it above the soggy wetlands, and continues
on towards Rohan.
name: Remains of a Road

desc:
A wide flat area opens up to the south, covered in soft grasslands. The road to
the south-east begins to gradually rise up onto a causeway, making its way
through the wetlands. This old route used to travel for hundreds of miles over
these lands, but it is now almost completely forgotten. To the east there is a
natural depression in the meadowland, the steep slope blocking movement in that
direction.
name: Remains of a Road

desc:
This depression is swift and sudden, almost invisible from most sides because of
the tall grass. The base of the hollow is covered with thick brush in parts,
extremely troublesome to move through. Goldenrod and other wild flowers grow on
the steeper sides, most likely where the sun hits during the day. To the west
and south, a road runs on a raised causeway well above you. The slope is steep
and full of loose gravel, making it impossible to climb.
name: A Deep Hollow in the Plains

desc:
The prairie slopes faintly downward to the south, a faint decline only visible
from a distance. There the land becomes wetter, with the poor natural drainage
making for a permanent semi-wetlands. Many wildflowers grow here on the grassy
slope, the land being fertile and well watered. West the plains dip away into a
natural bowl in the plains, while east the plains continue until they meet the
southern end of the Misty Mountains.
name: Low Prairie

desc:
A few stray arms of the blackberry bushes are invading these grasslands. The
vines grow very densely, creating a tangled web of thorns near impossible to cut
your way through. An easier route would be to walk around them to the south.
Here the signs of birds and other animal life are heard and often seen, these
lands being a fertile home for them. There is a faint trail here, headed south.
It appears to be made by wildlife, judging by the hoof prints.
name: Vale near the Brambles

desc:
This clearing is surrounded by thick impenetrable brambles and brush on all
sides but south, where a path cuts its way through. A single cherry tree stands
in the centre of the clearing, a solitary place giving a good view over the
nearby brush. The ground is covered in grass, cherry pits, and various animal
tracks.
name: A Small Clearing

desc:
Lush vines with ripe grapes are plentiful here. The rolls of vines are well
groomed and taken care of. The ground below is covered in a light brown dirt,
and there is not a weed to be seen. There are several breaks in the rolls of
vines, but not in the same place on each roll. Thus a bit of a maze is created
to travel through, but never enough to get lost in. The ground flows gently
downward in steps, lower to the south. West beyond the vines is a huge expanse
of brush and brambles, with razor sharp thorns that block the way.
name: In a Vineyard

desc:
The wheat is having trouble taking root in this bare rocky ground, which is
littered with small grey stones. There are a few large boulders to the north,
where they must have rolled down from the hillside. There is a dark tower on top
of the hill, looking out from its western end. To the east the outline of a
building can be seen, while west there is a stone wall enclosing a vineyard.
name: Sparse Fields of Wild Wheat

desc:
Apparently these fields were abandoned for some reason. The grain is not at all
healthy, or as well-tended as those in nearby fields. It may be that there is
too much wheat growing, and the farmer decided it wasn't worth the effort to
grow more. There are more abandoned fields in the north, and beyond them on the
distant horizon a hilly range sticks out west from the Misty Mountains.
name: Abandoned Wheat Fields

desc:
Some of the fields here have stalks of corn that are at least seven feet high,
waving with a life of their own in the wind. There are other fields where the
corn is on a different rotation, and the ears are far from harvestable. The
stalks of corn do not grow in even rolls, and are so tightly planted together
that manoeuvring around them is somewhat impeded. The corn closer to harvest
seems to be in the northern fields, and they block the view of what lies beyond
them. Bordering these fields on the west is a thorny mess of brambles and brush
that don't offer any path or route through.
name: Among the Corn Fields

desc:
The tunnel between the bushes twists and turns wildly here, but always seeming
to return to the same direction. There are several different natural tunnels
that lead off into the brush, but none run for any great distance. This path
appears to hold true to course though, leading somewhere through this maze.
name: Twisting Path through the Blackberry Bushes

desc:
Directly to the east is a mess of twisted blackberry bushes, their razor-like
thorns sharp enough to cut on contact. Some brave animal has tunnelled its way
through the brambles to some unknown place deep inside. Intertwined inside the
vicious thorns are various low trees, which seem to have had their life almost
completely choked out of them. Southwards the lands are covered in stagnant
water in parts, with a raised causeway running through them just visible on the
horizon.
name: Before a Blackberry Bramble

desc:
Just below the surface of the grass is about a half-inch of water. The earth,
already saturated, is too soaked to take in this last bit of water from the
recent rains. Always the water drains too slowly, the plains too flat and the
soil not absorbent enough to lose its watery cover. An ancient road borders
these wetlands to the south, built on a raised platform with small dikes under
it to drain the water. The Numenoreans built the causeway straight through the
wetlands, their engineering skill avoiding a large detour around the water.
name: Grassland Teeming with Water

desc:
The road continues along on a built-up causeway, well above the wetlands to the
south. There is a deep depression to the north, with some thick, tangled brush
congesting its base. Part of the old road has fallen away into it, leaving the
route barely passable. The surviving whole slabs on this road are few - most
broken into gravel by the weather long ago. Westwards the causeway gradually
disappears as the wetlands recede, the road inclining down to the plains.
name: Fragmented Road near Some Brush

desc:
To avoid flooding from the wet grasslands, this ancient trail continues along
its path on a causeway to the east of here. The rise is gradual, the line of the
road as straight as it was centuries ago. Still, the surface has suffered from
the lack of maintenance, becoming little more than a muddy track filled with
rough stone fragments. Here and there the road disappears from sight as it dips
into the undergrowth. Around the road the land is mainly flat grassy fields,
although a wide depression dips down to the north-east.
name: Remains of a Road

desc:
The meadow here slopes upwards towards the old road to the east. The ground is
covered in thick grass mainly, with a few spots of wildflowers growing nearer
the road. A route to the south leads over a hillside, while further south a
small stand of trees is discernible on the horizon.
name: A Gentle Slope near a Road

desc:
This slight rise gives a good view of the ruined road, which it overlooks to the
north-east. Surrounding here is a rich meadowland, verdant and flourishing with
thick, waving grass. To the southeast there is a stand of trees, barely visible
in the distance, bordering the wetlands. To the south is a steep and slippery
bank that looks unsafe to traverse.
name: A Slight Rise in the Meadow

desc:
This bluff is extremely difficult to traverse because of the countless small
screes. Going around it might be a better alternative than trying to climb, and
probably fall, down it. South looks the safest route down, with north and east
being more of a cliff than anything else.
name: A Steep Bluff in the Plains

desc:
The path here leads around the steep eastern slope of a bluff. The grass is
trampled quite heavily, and in parts there is nothing but dirt. By examination
of the tracks, it seems to be a trail made by a wild herd of animals. East and
south the hillside gradually flattens down onto the plains, which is mostly lush
green meadows stretching for miles. South, a long dotted line of trees stretches
for miles.
name: A Path on a Hillside

desc:
Bordering the remains of an ancient road, these fields are lush if somewhat
uniform. There are some small bushes and clusters of blackberry bushes, but
mostly the waving grass grows to the exclusion of all other undergrowth. The
land rises to the west up a gradual slope, the side of a steep bluff visible in
the distance.
name: Gentle Rolling Meadowland

desc:
The platform that this road sits on is rapidly eroding along its edges, leaving
it slippery and dangerous. Where half-upended flagstones do not threaten to tip
you over, the overgrown weeds may trip you up. The causeway itself has started
to seriously decay, and it will not be long before this part of the Great South
Road is only a memory as well. The lands surrounding it are open grasslands to
the north, with semi-swampland to the south.
name: Crumbling Remains of a Road

desc:
This is the highest place in the nearby lands, and visibility is good in every
direction. The ruined road runs on a bed of stone and earth for miles through
the wetlands, several yards above the water. Its age shows clearly, with large
parts having fallen away. Far to the north-east the Misty Mountains can be seen
rising in the sky with snow capped peaks, their stony might utterly dominating
the horizon.
name: Crumbling Remains of a Road

desc:
The blades of grass are slick with water here, the soil below almost completely
saturated. Poor natural drainage results in it pooling after the rains, making
almost a marsh in the grasslands. South and west of here runs an ancient road
atop a causeway, built to be safe from the frequent flooding. Its earthen sides
are covered in loose stones, and would be easy to slip on.
name: Wet Grasslands

desc:
The ground is covered in thick, short green grass. There are some bright spots
of yellow scattered among the grass which, upon closer examination, are tiny
buttercups. The calls of birds and the snuffling of animals can be heard to the
north, where some dense brambles block the way. A small, but well travelled path
is directly south of here, and some fields of grain directly east.
name: Short Grasslands Bordering a Path

desc:
These well-cultivated fields are in various rotations of growth, some closer to
harvest than others. Each field is about an acre in size and they rotate between
amber fields of wheat and the dark green plants of rye. Soon those closest to
harvest will make their way to the nearby Dunlending town. A farm house is
visible to the south-east, where several well-rutted paths lead.
name: Grain Fields

desc:
This field is covered in black dirt - it appears to have been burnt. Not even a
bright green leaf of any weed has sprouted here yet. Even though the dirt is
black, it is noticeable that the ground has been freshly ploughed. East and west
of here the fields have some crops in full growth. North-east there are some
trees, planted in even lines. The back wall of the farm house is south of here,
with several pieces of farm equipment near it.
name: A Field off Rotation

desc:
There are fields of tall wheat blocking the views south and north of here,
waving in the wind together like waves in the sea. The field grows shorter to
the east, revealing a fenced area. There are several animals grazing in this
enclosed field, near to a farmhouse. The entrance to the farmyard is just east
of here, surrounded by several buildings.
name: Path Close to a Farm

desc:
A barely noticeable path comes down from the old main road which lies directly
to the west, and leads eastward to a small farm. North of here there are fields
of short grasslands which are obviously tended, and beyond them some very thick
brush. South the causeway of the old road runs by, eroded over time but still
passable.
name: A Small Path

desc:
This ancient road carries on to the north-west on a raised causeway, but a
smaller path runs off to the east and leads to an old farm. Along the track
there are some farm fields which appear to be tended. On each side of the road
here is wetlands, perhaps the reason why the road is on a causeway. To the south
and east it dries out though, and the road levels to the plains.
name: The Tattered Remains of a Junction

desc:
The abandoned road, so eroded it is almost dangerous, leads slightly downwards
to the south-east, where the ground seems to become less wet. Here the road is
surrounded by stagnant wetlands, but the causeway keeps the road from flooding.
The soggy grasslands stretch for some distance to the south, the terrain being
nothing but endless reeds and water.
name: Crumbling Remains of a Road

desc:
The ground here is saturated with water, and it squishes out underfoot like a
wet sponge. The only grass that has taken root is a brittle, soft green; but it
is sparse. An ancient road skirts by to the north-east, avoiding flooding on its
raised causeway. Tiny dikes are built into it, draining rainwater off the road.
There is a stand of trees some way to the south-west, along the border of the
wetlands.
name: Soggy Grasslands

desc:
The ground here is covered in thick grass, turning as one in the wind. Patches
of wildflowers and daisies are interspersed here and there, but not in great
numbers. The grassland is bordered to the south by a stand of alder trees,
beyond which the terrain turns to wetlands. North-east an old road cuts through
the fields, rising up above the soggy terrain on an earthen causeway.
name: Abundant Grassland

desc:
The land begins to rise here, towards a raised bluff in the north-west. The
ground is covered in lush green grass, flattened in parts as if by a rampaging
herd of animals. It seems to be especially so to the north, where a trail made
by animal hooves is clearly visible on the hillside. South and east the ground
flattens out into open meadows.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
The meadow here surrounds the small hill to the north. It would make excellent
farm land, the ground being very fertile. Indeed the grass looks to be a bit
trampled in places, as if a herd or pack of animals had recently come through
here. To the south, the meadows continue, while to the north they rise up on the
sides of a bluff.
name: A Lush Green Meadow

desc:
Looking around here, a well-trodden track can be seen to the west, seemingly
left by wagons. The wheel-ruts run all the way into the yard, where the wagons
load with grain destined for a nearby Dunlending camp. There is an incredible
stench drifting in from the south, where an animal pen lies. East is a rose
garden, while north is the main farmhouse.
name: Farm Yard

desc:
This path passes through the soggy land, alongside a tiny stream. To the south,
the path acts almost as a border between the sweeping grasslands of the east and
the western wilderness. North-west and south the trail follows the trickle of
water through the southern lands of Enedwaith.
name: Soggy Trail
